page: Microsoft Azure D128alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Daldsv7 family, powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with x86_64 architecture, offering 128 dedicated vCPUs across 64 physical cores with multithreading running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU includes an L3 cache of 32 MB and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, alongside cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

This server is configured with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. This lower memory-to-vCPU ratio positions the instance as a compute-heavy option. For storage, it includes a substantial 7559 GB of local disk space and is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-throughput local I/O operations without relying solely on remote network volumes. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple vectors. The AMD EPYC processor achieves top 10% scores in both single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests, alongside exceptional memory bandwidth, reaching 12348.52 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 376.33 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. It also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in data compression, database operations, and static web serving, with Redis SET operations exceeding 200 million operations per second. Additionally, the CPU's vector extensions enable strong to top-tier LLM inference speeds, processing up to 5724.69 tokens per second on small models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D128alds_v7 offers high compute density and large local storage capacity at a lower memory footprint, making it a cost-effective choice for workloads that require massive parallel processing power but do not demand high RAM-to-vCPU ratios. Tradeoffs include the low memory-per-core allocation, which may bottleneck memory-bound applications. It is highly suited for compute-intensive tasks, high-throughput web serving, large-scale file compression, local database hosting, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D128alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Daldsv7 family, featuring 128 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. Built on x86_64 architecture with 64 physical cores, it includes 256.0 GB of RAM and a massive 7559 GB of local storage. Optimized for high-compute efficiency, the server lacks a GPU but compensates with top-tier benchmark performance, including top 10% results in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and Redis database operations. The CPU supports AVX-512 and cryptography extensions, facilitating rapid data compression and CPU-based LLM inference. This configuration is highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy, storage-reliant workloads that do not require extensive memory capacity, making it an excellent fit for high-throughput web serving, parallel batch processing, and local database management.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D128alds_v7 is a compute-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs on a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC processor, 256 GB of RAM, and 7559 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D128alds_v7 is a compute-optimized server with 128 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 7559 GB local storage for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute platform combining 128 AMD EPYC vCPUs with massive local storage for parallel processing and high-throughput workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor
256 GB of RAM providing a compute-focused 2.0 GB per core ratio
7559 GB of high-capacity local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth in benchmark testing
Ideal for compute-heavy workloads, database operations, and CPU-based inference
